title: Monoalphabetic Cipher Encryption Process
description: A visual breakdown of how a monoalphabetic substitution cipher works, showing the relationship between the plaintext alphabet, the cipher alphabet, and the encryption/decryption process.
relationships: Plaintext Alphabet is mapped one-to-one to Cipher Alphabet to form the key
Encryption Function uses the key to substitute each plaintext character with its cipher equivalent
Decryption Function uses the same key to reverse the substitution and recover the original plaintext
